mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) captures aggregate consumer demand strength; higher demand directly lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS changes and 20-day forward returns remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the positive return hit rate falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 3.5% signals sufficiently restrictive monetary policy that raises borrowing costs and compresses equity valuation multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: In triggered periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.5%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising unemployment alongside a restrictive federal funds rate signals weakening labor markets without monetary accommodation, amplifying recession risk and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 3 months while FEDFUNDS > 3.0%
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 20-day returns in at least 50% of occurrences, or the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power (incremental R-squared < 0.01) versus main effects alone.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recovery phases, month-over-month accelerations in industrial production reflect rebounding factory utilization and inventory restocking that outpaces expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: In identified post-recession recovery months, INDPRO acceleration coincides with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of instances, or the strategy underperforms the unconditional market return (information ratio < 0.2).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth (GDPC1) above its trailing two-year trend indicates broad-based aggregate demand expansion that lifts corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 changes and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained three-month contraction in industrial production signals deteriorating manufacturing output and supply-side disinvestment, leading to negative earnings surprises and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Triggered instances with three consecutive monthly INDPRO declines fail to produce negative 20-day returns in at least 55% of cases, or the mean forward return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding 3.5% year-over-year erodes real corporate margins and prompts expectations of tighter monetary policy, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: During triggered high-inflation periods, 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month gains in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal expanding labor income and household spending power, which flows into higher corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS changes and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the positive return hit rate is below 55%.
